title: Poincianella myabensis
taxonomy: Poincianella myabensis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It is native to Australia, specifically in the Northern Territory and Queensland. The plant is part of the genus Poincianella, which is a group of flowering plants that are similar to Poinciana. The classification of Poincianella myabensis is further divided into the family Fabaceae, order Fabales, and subclass Rosidae. The specific classification of the plant is essential for understanding its characteristics and relationships within the plant kingdom.

intro: Poincianella myabensis, also known as the Myalla tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include the treatment of various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities. The bioactive constituents of Poincianella myabensis include flavonoids, terpenoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects.
